Identity.History: Trading posts and trade routes linked ancient civilizations across land and sea. Merchants carried metals, grain, wine, textiles, spices, incense, horses, slaves, luxury goods, and ideas. Overland routes connected Mesopotamia, Iran, India, China, Arabia, and the Mediterranean, while sea routes crossed the Red Sea, Persian Gulf, Indian Ocean, and Mediterranean. Trading posts served as markets, warehouses, customs points, and diplomatic contact zones. They spread wealth, religions, technologies, scripts, art styles, and diseases, making trade a major engine of ancient globalization.
